title: Org-Wide Memory index record v1
description: OWM-02: the SOLE structural source of truth for one Org-Wide Memory index record (ADR 0019). Every record is a DERIVED VIEW over repo-resident memory bytes, carrying a {repo, commit_sha, path, source_line} back-pointer to the authoritative source — never a second store of truth. Additive-only evolution (mirrors the manifest schema): unknown fields are ignored; a major schema_version bump forces a full index rebuild. The completeness/extraction rules (which fields each kind carries) live in the extractor, not here — this schema enforces only structural shape so a partially-extracted or unparseable record is still schema-valid and never silently dropped.

kind.description: The memory-class this record was extracted as (OWM-01), plus the two non-content edge kinds the crawler emits so an unparseable file or an unreachable/oversized repo is recorded, never dropped.

id.description: Stable within-repo identity (kebab name for adr/glossary; DL-### for decision-log; behaviour/journey id for manifest; path-derived otherwise). Reused from the source, never minted.

org_id.description: Stable CROSS-repo identity = <repo-slug>:<kind>:<kebab-name>. Disambiguated by repo so the same kebab name in two repos is two records, not a merge.

repo.description: Repo slug this record's bytes live in (the first segment of org_id). The ACL / allow-list enforcement key (OWM-11 refuse-by-default).

commit_sha.description: The pinned ref the crawler read this record's bytes at (OWM-04 cache-key). For crawl_error records that failed before a ref was resolvable this may be the empty string.

path.description: Repo-relative path of the source file the bytes came from — the back-pointer target so a human/agent opens the authoritative file.

source_line.description: 1-based line the record anchors to in path, when known (glossary term line, DL-### line). Omitted when not line-anchored.

title.description: Human title/label (ADR H1, glossary term, behaviour title, manifest spec title, DL summary).

status.description: adr: status frontmatter (accepted | agent-decided). manifest: completeness. unparseable/crawl_error: the failure category. Omitted when not applicable.

body_excerpt.description: A bounded prose excerpt for full-text retrieval (FTS5). For crawl_error/unparseable this carries the error message + validator exit code.

aliases.description: Rejected synonyms that must resolve to this record (glossary _Avoid_ list). Empty array when none — the 'told twice' killer for OWM-06 resolve.

supersedes.description: org_id / kebab-name this record supersedes (ADR chain), when declared.

superseded_by.description: org_id / kebab-name that supersedes this record (ADR superseded-by / amended-by), when declared. A record carrying this is returned FLAGGED, never as live truth (OWM-06).

spec_hash.description: manifest spec_hash when the source is a complete Verification Manifest.

error_code.description: For unparseable/crawl_error records: the machine code (validator exit 4/5, memory-surface-oversized, repo-unreachable) so coverage (OWM-08) can name WHY the index cannot answer.
